duplicate_boundary_checks.result: Hardware layer already cited the Atlas 300I A2 raw product-page capture but listed it as an unresolved multi-variant row. Parent-14 adds a resolved per-memory-variant slice and removes Atlas 300I A2 from that unresolved multi-variant gap.
Added one known_source entry for parent-14 Huawei Atlas 300I A2 variant fields without mutating unrelated blocked_items or non-hardware coverage gaps.
No pre-existing Huawei Atlas 300I A2 SKU row; added huawei-atlas-300i-a2-32gb and huawei-atlas-300i-a2-64gb tied to the existing huawei-atlas-300i-a2-product-page source.
No pre-existing Atlas 300I A2 observations; added observations only for form_factor, int8_tops, fp16_tflops, memory_capacity, memory_bandwidth, host_interface, and tdp for each memory variant.
No pre-existing Atlas 300I A2 resolved fields; added two resolved specs whose fields point to matching per-variant observations.
Existing huawei-atlas-300i-a2-product-page source registry entry was reused; the source registry was not modified.

Existing Atlas 300I A2 raw comparison row now links to resolved specs and explicitly separates 32 GB and 64 GB memory variants while keeping CPU, cooling, fan, dimension, operating-temperature, benchmark, production-operation, and aggregate claims outside resolved fields.
Catalog coverage text now treats Atlas 300I A2 as two bounded structured product rows and records non-promoted source-visible boundaries.

gap_reason: The local corpus already contained an official product-specific Huawei Atlas 300I A2 capture with source-visible, schema-supported form factor, INT8, FP16, memory-capacity, memory-bandwidth, host-interface, and maximum-power fields. The source exposes memory as two distinct on-card configurations, so the safe catalog boundary is two explicit variant rows rather than one blended row.

schema_boundary.source_value: The source lists Atlas 300I A2 on-card memory as 32 GB with 0.8 TB/s bandwidth and 64 GB with 1.6 TB/s bandwidth.
schema_boundary.boundary: Two explicit SKU rows are promoted so memory_capacity and memory_bandwidth are not blended into one ambiguous row.

schema_boundary.source_value: The source lists 560 TOPS INT8 and 280 TFLOPS FP16 for Atlas 300I A2 without tying different compute peaks to the two memory configurations.
schema_boundary.boundary: The shared compute values are repeated on both explicit memory variants because both rows remain within the same product-page boundary.

schema_boundary.source_value: The source lists CPU, passive air cooling, fan modules, operating temperature, and dimensions.
schema_boundary.boundary: No CPU, cooling, fan, dimension, or operating-temperature field is promoted because those fields are outside the task scope or absent from the current catalog schema.

schema_boundary.source_value: The source does not expose memory type/generation, benchmark result, production-operation proof, service SLO, training throughput, inference throughput, ranking, or aggregate-system value for Atlas 300I A2.
schema_boundary.boundary: No memory_type, benchmark, production-operation, service-SLO, training-throughput, inference-throughput, ranking, or aggregate-system claim is promoted.

non_promoted_boundaries: No Huawei Atlas 800T A3, Huawei aggregate system, server drawer, CPU, fan, cooling, dimension, operating-temperature, benchmark, production-operation, service-SLO, training-throughput, inference-throughput, pricing, ranking, or deployment claim was promoted as a resolved catalog field.
No Atlas 300I A2 memory_capacity or memory_bandwidth value was collapsed across 32 GB and 64 GB configurations.

No H200, AMD MI325X, Intel Gaudi 3, Cambricon, Kunlunxin, Biren, Corigine, Resnics, Yusur, Asterfusion, Iluvatar, MetaX, AWS Trn2, Huawei Atlas 800T A3, Kunlunxin R480-X8, GB200/GB300, or other unrelated row was intentionally modified by this task.
No new external source was fetched, and no crawler subscriptions or Domain Channels were changed.
